message:
fix(core): flush retained autocommit on delegated attached-schema CTE reads

`execute_statement_with_materialized_temp_tables` is invoked by
`execute_with_ctes` when a WITH-bearing SELECT's stripped body refers
only to an attached schema (e.g. `WITH RECURSIVE t(x) AS (...) SELECT
SUM(x) FROM aux.t;`). The helper bypasses the normal statement dispatch
path by calling `execute_select_via_memdb_fallback` directly on the
attached child connection.

The attached `:memory:` child's prior parent-dispatched writes (INSERT
INTO aux.t ...) are persisted via a retained autocommit transaction —
the same mechanism that batches autocommit writes on a regular
connection. On the normal dispatch path, a subsequent SELECT checks
`retained_autocommit_has_dirty_overlap` and calls
`flush_retained_autocommit_txn_for_read` before reading. The helper
skipped that gate, so `reload_memdb_from_pager` ran against the pager's
pre-write committed state and returned zero rows for the stripped
query.

Root cause: when the outer connection delegates an attached-target
SELECT inside a WITH clause, the child's uncommitted autocommit writes
are not visible to `reload_memdb_from_pager`, and the delegation helper
has no pre-read flush to bridge the gap. Prior patches (mangle CTE
name, force unconditional reload) worked around adjacent symptoms but
did not address the uncommitted-writer gap.

Fix: before `refresh_memdb_from_cached_write_txn_if_stale` /
`reload_memdb_from_pager` inside
`execute_statement_with_materialized_temp_tables`, flush any retained
autocommit txn so its writes commit to the pager. The subsequent
reload then sees the complete committed state.

Fixes: test_recursive_cte_sum_consumer_does_not_confuse_attached_table_with_cte
Also incidentally fixes: test_window_eval_trace_span_counter_records_enabled_debug_spans
Regressions: none (the three pre-existing attached-compound/schema-only
failures are unchanged vs. baseline).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
